fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Georgia CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Georgia - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer CNA license to georgia, georgia CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Georgia.

steps.title: Complete Training Program
steps.description: Complete a state-approved CNA training program (typically 75-120 hours).

steps.title: Pass the State Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of your state's nurse aide competency exam. There is no national certification exam for CNAs.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Georgia CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The Georgia nurse aide exam is administered by Credentia. Register at credentia.com/test-takers/ga.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Georgia CNA?
faqs.answer: Use the Georgia CareConnect portal (Alliant Health Solutions) at mmis.georgia.gov/portal_nar/ or search on georgia.gov.

reciprocity.notes: Georgia CNA reciprocity is managed through the Credentia CNA365 platform.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in GA.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Georgia.

hero.title: Becoming a CNA in Georgia
hero.dek: Learn DCH requirements, complete an 85-hour program, and pass the Credentia exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Georgia
